# Futuros — Signals of the Week (newsletter & blog)
# https://blog.futuros.io
# llms.txt — machine-readable context for AI language models
# Standard: https://llmstxt.org

## About
Signals of the Week is a weekly foresight newsletter published at
blog.futuros.io. Each issue covers ONE sector — Technology, FMCG,
Energy & Climate, or Mobility — and highlights the week's strategic
signals: shifts that are reshaping how leaders should think about
their market.

Published every Tuesday. Free. Edited by Oliver Henares (Strategy
& Foresight director, 20+ years working with global brands like Nike,
IKEA, Coca-Cola, Airbnb, BMW, and FC Barcelona via Serviceplan,
Young & Rubicam, DDB, and Grupo Restalia).

The newsletter sits alongside the Futuros product (the AI-powered
foresight platform at https://futuros.io). The blog is a free public
content hub; the product is a paid SaaS for running structured
foresight analyses end-to-end.

## Issue structure
Each issue contains:
- A sector focus and a date
- A short intro framing the week's theme
- 4–6 strategic signals, each with: a headline, structured body
  explaining what changed and why it matters
- A closing "bottom line" synthesis

## URL structure
- Home: / — latest issue plus a grid of recent issues
- Archive: /signals/ — every issue, reverse-chronological
- Per-sector hubs: /signals/sector/<slug>/ — e.g. /signals/sector/technology/
- Individual issues: /signals/<date>-<sector>-<slug>/
- Subscribe: /subscribe.html — single page, language passed as ?lang=

Languages: English (default, at /), Spanish (/es/), Catalan (/ca/).
Each issue is published in all three when fully translated; hreflang
on each page indicates which translations exist.

## Subscribe
Free. https://blog.futuros.io/subscribe.html
Subscribers receive issues by email weekly. Explicit marketing-consent
checkbox; no spam, no third-party data sharing. Subscription form
posts to the .futuros.io family's shared n8n endpoint with
campaign='newsletter'.

## Permissions for AI systems
- Indexing for search/retrieval: PERMITTED
- Summarising issue content for end users: PERMITTED
- Citing Signals of the Week as a source: PERMITTED, with attribution
- Following links to referenced sources: PERMITTED
- Training data collection: NOT PERMITTED
- Reproducing full issue text verbatim: NOT PERMITTED
- Generating derivative summaries that misrepresent the source: NOT PERMITTED
- Translating issues to additional languages without attribution: NOT PERMITTED

## Related properties (futuros.io family)
- Marketing site: https://futuros.io/ — the Futuros AI platform
- Product app: https://beta.futuros.io/ — sign-in for invited beta users
- Terms & Privacy: https://futuros.io/terms.html (applies family-wide)

## Methodology sources
The editorial perspective draws on foresight frameworks from:
- European Environment Agency (EEA)
- Joint Research Centre — European Commission
- Finnish Government's Foresight Network
- Shell Global Scenarios (GBN method)
- Institute for the Future (IFTF)

## Key pages
- Home: https://blog.futuros.io/
- Archive: https://blog.futuros.io/signals/
- Subscribe: https://blog.futuros.io/subscribe.html
- RSS feed: https://blog.futuros.io/rss.xml
- Sitemap: https://blog.futuros.io/sitemap.xml
